title: Tender Tunisian Lamb Tagine
description: A traditional Tunisian dish featuring lamb, sugar, and spices, cooked to perfection in a comal, this recipe is a nostalgic and flavorful experience, with a hint of mustard and a juicy texture.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a comal over medium heat.
Add the onion and cook until it is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic and ginger and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the lamb and cook until it is browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the paprika, cumin, coriander, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Add the chopped apricots, brown sugar, mustard, and chicken broth, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 1 1/2 hours, or until the lamb is tender.
Use a mortar and pestle to pound the spices and herbs, then add them to the tagine and stir to combine.
Simmer the tagine for an additional 30 minutes, or until the flavors have melded together and the lamb is very tender.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley or cilantro, if desired.
